picks.reasoning: PSG won the first leg AND benefited from Chelsea's lineup being leaked beforehand — which means tonight Chelsea face them without that tactical gift. Rosenior's side now need to chase the game, opening up space for PSG on the counter. A team that just had their homework handed to them winning at a chaotic Stamford Bridge? At 3.0, this is value.

Leg 1 ended with Arsenal nicking an equaliser via a penalty that Leverkusen's manager is still furious about. Tonight Leverkusen arrive at the Emirates with a point to prove, a set-piece arsenal of their own (their coach got an earful about it), and nothing to lose. Arsenal at 1.28 to win feels heroically optimistic. A draw at 5.75 — sending it to extra time — is structurally underpriced.

Pep's City are described as 'unbalanced and clinging to faint title hopes' — not exactly the war cry of a team primed for European glory. Real Madrid have won this competition 15 times. They've beaten City at the Etihad before when they had no right to. At 5.75 against a City side with its mind elsewhere, Los Blancos are worth a dart.
